steps.title: Identify what the blank must supply
steps.content: The sentence has a compound subject: his energetic gestures and his habit of barreling after a hit ball to see if it would land out of ______. This compound subject needs a main finite verb to complete the clause and show what those gestures and habits accomplished.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘bounds helped’: "Helped" is a finite past-tense verb that governs the compound subject. "Helped transform" (helped + infinitive) is a standard construction, giving the sentence a complete main clause. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘bounds, helping’: The comma turns "helping transform" into a participial phrase, leaving no main verb — the sentence becomes a fragment. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘bounds that helped’: Introduces a relative clause modifying "bounds" with an ambiguous antecedent, and still leaves the main clause without a verb. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘bounds to help’: Creates an infinitive phrase that doesn't fit the sentence's grammatical structure — the compound subject has no finite verb. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
